name: Split Pea Soup with Croutons
recipeIngredient: 1 leek (white part only), cleaned and sliced
Sea salt and pepper to taste
6 slices whole wheat bread, cut into cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat safflower oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add onions and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
3. Add garlic and cook for an additional minute.
4. Stir in carrots and leeks, cooking for another 3-4 minutes.
5. Add the split peas, water, bay leaf, salt, and pepper to taste. Bring mixture to a boil.
6. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1 hour or until peas are tender.
7. Remove the bay leaf and puree half of the soup in a blender or through a food mill.
8. Return the puréed portion back to the pot and adjust seasonings if needed.
9. In a separate pan, melt butter over medium heat.
10. Add garlic and bread cubes, cooking until the cubes are golden brown (this can be done in advance).
11. Remove from heat and serve the soup hot, topping each bowl with croutons.
